summary: Phase 2 (P1) canonical party identity verified end-to-end. Ran the pre-existing spec suite (test_p1_party_auto_create.py, 14 tests) plus a new supplementary suite (test_p1_party_review_extras.py, 6 tests) covering the two extra review items (system party rename block + GET system party). All 20 tests pass serially.

minor.issue: Flaky under pytest-xdist parallel execution (default per /app/backend/pytest.ini): TestConcurrency runs in a sibling worker and inserts a party between the two /party-migration/run calls, causing parties_created to differ by 1. Serial run passes cleanly. Recommend marking this test with @pytest.mark.serial or using a filelock, or asserting `abs(second - first) <= <expected concurrent inserts>`. Not a backend regression.

action_items: Optional: mark test_migration_is_idempotent as serial (or add worker-lock) so the default xdist run is deterministic. All functional behavior is correct.
critical_code_review_comments: Rename endpoint /api/parties/{pid}/rename accepts key `display_name` (Pydantic body). Documenting for future callers — the review request text used `new_name`; endpoint uses `display_name`. Consider accepting both aliases if `new_name` is documented anywhere externally.

Migration idempotency depends on no concurrent writers between runs. Consider taking a short read-lock or a monotonic 'last_scan_at' watermark so parallel writers do not cause counters to drift across identical migration runs.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates a UUID-suffixed party row (P2-Cust-*, P2-Vend-*, P2-VendPur-*, P2-CustOrd-*, P2-Idem-*, Zahir-*, P2-Rename-*, P2-Dup-*, P2-Concurrent-*, P1-Ven-*, P1-VenRen-*). These are cheap party rows and are not cleaned up (party-migration idempotency means they will not multiply on rerun).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both P1 test files (test_p1_party_auto_create.py + test_p1_party_review_extras.py) must be run with pytest-xdist disabled (e.g. `pytest -o addopts=''`) to avoid the known flake in TestMigration::test_migration_is_idempotent caused by TestConcurrency executing on a sibling worker. Backend is externally reachable at REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env but tests intentionally hit http://localhost:8001/api because that is the direct backend port (both work).
rca of the issue: Under xdist parallel scheduling, TestMigration and TestConcurrency get scheduled on different workers. TestConcurrency issues 6 parallel customer-payment POSTs that create a brand-new party. If those POSTs land between TestMigration's two consecutive /party-migration/run calls, parties_created reflects that extra party on the second call, so `first == second` fails. Repro: `pytest backend/tests/test_p1_party_auto_create.py` (default addopts uses -n auto). Mitigation: run serially with `-o addopts=''`, or add a serial marker, or change the assertion to `assert 0 <= (second - first) <= <expected concurrent-test insertions>`.
